rule: Guidance for DatasetExaminer warning ("Mean feature skewness=3.6 is high. Log-transform recommended before clustering."): yes apply log-transforms
Guidance for DatasetExaminer warning ("Mean feature skewness=3.6 is high. Log-transform recommended before clustering."): yes apply what you suggested
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Cluster 1 has 92.4% of data (>40% threshold)"): recluster this too big cluster to a smaller size
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Cluster 1 has 92.4% of data (>40% threshold)"): recluster this big cluster to proper size, you can decide after analyse it
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Silhouette=0.127 < 0.15 — clusters overlap; interpretability may be low. Consider reviewing feature selection or k."): go back to feature selection
Guidance for PersonaNamer warning ("Must-have cluster type(s) not found in any persona name/description: ['travellers']"): try more k
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Cluster 4 has 40.9% of data (>40% threshold)"): recluster that cluster 4, to more clusters
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Silhouette=0.145 < 0.15 — clusters overlap; interpretability may be low. Consider reviewing feature selection or k."): let it pass

before.description: These 24 people spend 3–3.7x the average on miscellaneous purchases (both online and in-store) and 2.65x the average on entertainment. They live in very small towns despite their high spending, and they barely travel at all. Their money goes into local entertainment and diverse, large purchases rather than travel.
before.name: Entertainment & Miscellaneous Big Spenders
before.tagline: They drop large sums on fun and hard-to-categorise purchases
traits: Miscellaneous spending is over 3x the average
Entertainment spending is nearly 3x above average
Very low travel spending — homebodies who spend locally
Live in tiny towns despite very high expenditure
Diverse, high-value purchases across categories

before.description: All 112 people in this cluster are female (0% male), and they are the single biggest grocery buyers in the entire dataset — spending 2.7–2.8x the average on both online and in-store groceries. Their grocery transaction counts are also 2.5x above average, meaning they shop for food very frequently and in large amounts.
before.name: Grocery-Obsessed Bulk Buyers
before.tagline: They buy groceries constantly and in very large quantities
traits: 100% female — the only all-female cluster
Grocery spending is nearly 3x above average
Shops for groceries very frequently — over 2.5x the average count
Large in-store and online grocery volumes
Low gas spending — may rely on delivery or live close to stores

target_cluster_name: NOx Alarm with Elevated Benzene & Warmth
hint: what does it mean Mild but then Spike? isn't that contradict?

before.name: High NOx Sensor Alert, Mild Benzene Spike

after.name: NOx Alarm with Elevated Benzene & Warmth

target_cluster_name: NOx Alarm with Elevated Benzene & Warmth

before.description: This cluster is defined by an extraordinarily high rate of NOx sensor alerts — nearly 4 times the overall average — meaning almost every reading in this group triggers a warning. On top of that, benzene (C6H6) levels are nearly twice the typical amount, and temperatures are noticeably warmer than average. Humidity, on the other hand, sits slightly below average, suggesting dry, warm, polluted air conditions.
before.name: NOx Alarm with Elevated Benzene & Warmth
before.tagline: Sensors screaming NOx, benzene running high, and temperatures above the norm
traits: NOx sensor triggers an alert almost every single reading (99.7% of the time)
Benzene concentrations are nearly double the overall average
Temperatures are well above typical — think warm days rather than mild ones
Air humidity is slightly lower than usual — drier conditions
A consistent pattern of warm, dry air loaded with both NOx and benzene pollution

rule: Guidance for Clusterer warning (""): lower than 60% is allowed
Guidance for FeatureSelector warning ("Some features still have VIF > 12.0 (max=12.3)."): yes, do it as you think
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Cluster 4 has 50.9% of data (>40% threshold)"): 50% is ok
Guidance for Clusterer warning ("Silhouette=0.093 < target 0.50 — orchestrator will reselect features (or escalate after 3 consecutive misses)."): yes reselect featues, the Silhouette is way to low
Guidance for Classifier warning ("CV macro-F1=0.454 below threshold 0.7. Clusters may not be well-separated."): re-select the features
